title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: Market Recap for Friday, May 23, 2026 (Based on May 22, 2026 Closing Data)

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Extend Winning Streak Amid Strong Earnings, Consumer Sentiment Dips
items.summary: U.S. equities closed out an eighth consecutive week of gains, the longest such streak since 2023, with major indices nearing all-time highs. This rally is largely fueled by a robust corporate earnings season. However, a survey indicates U.S. consumer sentiment has fallen to a record low, primarily due to inflation concerns exacerbated by the war in Iran. AI momentum and a strong healthcare sector are key drivers, while the utilities sector faces headwinds.

items.headline: Dell Technologies
items.summary: Dell Technologies surged on anticipation of strong earnings driven by confidence in the AI boom. Analysts expect a remarkable 52% annual revenue growth.

items.headline: Estee Lauder
items.summary: Estee Lauder jumped after announcing it was no longer considering a merger with Puig.

items.headline: Zoom Communications
items.summary: Zoom Communications rose after delivering better-than-expected profit reports for the latest quarter.

items.headline: Ross Stores
items.summary: Ross Stores helped drive the market higher after reporting profit and revenue that cleared analysts' expectations, noting strong customer traffic.

items.headline: NVIDIA Corp
items.summary: Nvidia experienced a slight decline. While its strong earnings failed to significantly ignite the broader tech sector, market participants are reassessing AI-related memory demand.

items.headline: Alphabet (Google)
items.summary: Alphabet (Google) saw a modest decrease in its stock price.

items.headline: Sector Spotlight: Tech and Healthcare Lead, Utilities Lag
items.summary: The technology sector has shown a strong resurgence in May, up 10.6% for the month, now being the second-best performing sector year-to-date with a 22.3% return, behind energy (34.5%). Healthcare also had its best week in six months, surging over 3%. In contrast, the utilities sector has slid 4.9% in May, possibly due to concerns about AI data centers' impact on electric grids.

items.headline: Consumer Sentiment Hits Record Low Amid Inflation Fears; New Fed Chair Takes Oath
items.summary: U.S. consumer sentiment has fallen to a record low, with households increasingly worried about inflation, forecasting it to worsen to 4.8% in the coming 12 months. This sentiment is largely influenced by expensive oil prices due to the ongoing war in Iran. Kevin Warsh is expected to take the oath as the new Federal Reserve chairman today, facing these economic challenges. The 10-year Treasury yield dropped to 4.56%.

items.headline: AI Momentum Continues to Accelerate, Driving Tech Growth
items.summary: The artificial intelligence (AI) trend continues to be a significant driver in the market. Companies like Dell Technologies are experiencing surges due to confidence in the AI boom, particularly with the transition towards generative AI applications. Institutional money is reportedly piling into AI plays, reinforcing the sector's strength.

items.headline: Strong Q1 Earnings Season Underpins Market Rally
items.summary: The Q1 2026 earnings season has been robust, with 84% of S&P 500 companies reporting a positive EPS surprise and 81% reporting a positive revenue surprise. The blended earnings growth rate for the S&P 500 is 28.4%, marking the highest growth rate since Q4 2021. Companies like Ross Stores, Estee Lauder, Workday, and Zoom Communications exceeded analyst expectations.
